entity.summary: Defunct Canadian centralized cryptocurrency exchange that entered creditor protection in February 2019 after founder Gerald Cotten's death and missing-customer-funds crisis, later moving into bankruptcy. HEI records QuadrigaCX as a dead event-backed exchange with insolvency and regulatory-review history.
entity.notes: Added as a fresh-discovery event-backed record rather than from the verified-unadded 1000-row backlog. QuadrigaCX is treated as dead because the original exchange entered creditor protection, then bankruptcy, and did not resume as the original exchange service. Fraud descriptions are attributed to OSC and reporting sources.

events.title: QuadrigaCX received creditor protection after founder death and missing-funds crisis
events.description: QuadrigaCX sought and received creditor protection in Nova Scotia after founder Gerald Cotten's death and the reported inability to access large amounts of customer cryptocurrency.
events.notes: This marks the practical end of the original exchange service timeline.

events.title: QuadrigaCX moved into bankruptcy proceedings
events.description: QuadrigaCX moved from creditor-protection proceedings into bankruptcy proceedings in April 2019 after recovery and restructuring prospects became remote.
events.notes: Bankruptcy event is recorded separately from the February 2019 creditor-protection event.

events.title: OSC staff review concluded Quadriga operated as a fraud
events.description: The Ontario Securities Commission staff review concluded that Quadriga's collapse resulted from fraud by Gerald Cotten and described the case as an old-fashioned fraud wrapped in modern technology.
events.notes: Fraud characterization is attributed to the OSC staff review and reporting sources.

events.title: QuadrigaCX added as fresh-discovery historical exchange
events.description: QuadrigaCX was added as a fresh-discovery historical exchange record because of strong event-backed public evidence and historical significance.
events.notes: No verified-unadded backlog row was consumed.

evidence.title: A Crypto Exchange CEO Dies-With the Only Key to $137 Million
evidence.notes: Report on Cotten's death, missing access to funds, and creditor-protection context.

evidence.title: The QuadrigaCX crypto mystery deepens as wallets turn up empty
evidence.notes: Follow-up reporting on court-appointed monitor findings and empty wallets.
